chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings

text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : «فِي حَمَامِ مَكَّةَ اَلطَّيْرُ اَلْأَهْلِيُّ مِنْ حَمَامِ اَلْحَرَمِ مَنْ ذَبَحَ مِنْهُ طَيْراً فَعَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَتَصَدَّقَ بِصَدَقَةٍ أَفْضَلَ مِنْ ثَمَنِهِ فَإِنْ كَانَ مُحْرِماً فَشَاةٌ عَنْ كُلِّ طَيْرٍ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2366 - Al-Nadr narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan:
I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say: "For the pigeons of Mecca and the domesticated birds from the pigeons of the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram), whoever slaughters a bird from them must give charity greater than its value. If the person was in the state of Ihram, then a sheep is required for each bird."
